Add examples and documentation for streaming-rtl library

- ready_valid_basics.md: handshake rules, timing diagrams, correct/incorrect examples
- buffering_patterns.md: buffer type selection guide, depth sizing, flag usage
- arbitration_patterns.md: fixed-priority vs round-robin, mux/demux, merge vs join
- packet_streaming.md: frame structure, header workflows, counters, timeout/idle
- async_fifo_notes.md: Gray-code theory, SYNC_STAGES, CDC sizing, reset sequence

commit.message:
Update FLL tournament analyzer skill with lessons learned

Key additions from Adventist Robotics Championship analysis:
- Solo matches as alternative to surrogates for odd team counts
- Exhibition/bonus runs after official matches (don't include in tables)
- Cross-round matches (one match scoring for different rounds per team)
- False start detection (reset/stop/sorry after countdown)
- Distinguishing START vs END countdowns (five-four before = END)
- Announcer overlap (announces next match during current)
- VTT caption deduplication technique
- Game element vs team name confusion warning
- Awards finalists pattern (2 finalists + 1 winner)
- Coach Mentor and Rising All-Star award notes

commit.message:
Debugger fixes (#173)

* Fix keyboard input not working until F5 in debugger

Intercept ShortcutOverride events for arrow/A keys in the application
event filter so focused widgets (QTreeView, QComboBox) cannot claim
them for their own navigation, preventing the subsequent KeyPress from
reaching our game controls.

Also give the game view StrongFocus and shift focus to it after bridge
creation so the model browser tree doesn't hold focus unnecessarily.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove 'Scenario:' label from debugger toolbar

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add GAE advantage column to debugger step history

Add an Advantage column between Action and Reward in the step history
tree. On each step, extract the model's value estimate and recompute
GAE advantages for all buffered steps using the standard formula:
  δ_t = r_t + γ·V(s_{t+1})·mask - V(s_t)
  A_t = δ_t + γ·λ·mask·A_{t+1}

Toolbar spinboxes for γ and λ default to the training hyperparameters
from ml_ppo.py (0.99, 0.95). Changing them triggers recomputation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add multi-select and Ctrl+C copy to step history

Change selection mode from SingleSelection to ExtendedSelection so
multiple rows can be selected with Shift/Ctrl+click. Ctrl+C copies
all selected rows as tab-separated text (all columns) to the clipboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Renormalize probability column and add unmasked column

PROBABILITY column now excludes masked actions when computing
percentages, so visible values always sum to 100%. New UNMASKED
column shows raw softmax probabilities from the model.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Load State to debugger File menu

File > Load State (Ctrl+L) opens a .state file picker, resets the
environment, injects the loaded emulator state bytes, and steps a
noop to re-derive all wrapper state. The scenario remains active
for critics, end conditions, and objectives.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix multihead direction mask ignoring action constraints

flat_mask_to_multihead() was hardcoding the direction mask to all True,
meaning multihead models could sample any direction regardless of which
directions were actually valid. This affected training, evaluation, and
the debugger equally.

The direction mask is now the union of valid directions across all valid
action types. A direction is only unmasked if at least one enabled action
type allows it. This prevents e.g. MOVE N when only MOVE S is walkable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Print exit criteria metric value after each training leg

After each scenario in a training circuit, print the last observed
value of the exit criteria metric alongside the target threshold and
whether it was reached. Helps diagnose training progress without
needing to check tensorboard.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add descriptive tooltips to γ and λ labels and spinboxes

- Merge comparisons with 'not in' (R1714)

Add framing/packet RTL modules to streaming-rtl library

Add 8 synthesizable SystemVerilog modules under rtl/framing/:

- stream_packetizer: inserts m_last every PACKET_LEN beats on a
  continuous stream (no s_last input); zero-latency pass-through
  with a registered beat counter.

- stream_depacketizer: absorbs s_last to produce a continuous
  output stream (m_last always 0); tracks frame_count (32-bit).

- stream_frame_length_counter: transparent pass-through; count
  output (clog2(MAX_LEN+1) bits, 1-indexed) equals frame length
  when m_last is asserted.

- stream_frame_detector: transparent pass-through; sof pulses on
  the first accepted beat of each frame, eof on the last.

- stream_last_inserter: generates m_last periodically every
  COUNT_BEATS or when the insert_last sideband is asserted on a
  valid beat; no s_last input.

- stream_last_remover: purely combinatorial; absorbs s_last and
  ties m_last to 0; clk/rst_n present for interface consistency.

- stream_header_inserter: 3-state FSM (S_HDR_WAIT → S_HDR_SEND
  → S_PAYLOAD); stalls payload, handshakes on hdr sideband,
  outputs HEADER_BEATS from captured header, then passes payload
  with m_last following s_last.

- stream_header_stripper: 2-state FSM (S_HDR_CAP → S_PAYLOAD);
  captures first HEADER_BEATS beats without forwarding, pulses
  hdr_valid one registered cycle after full header is captured,
  then passes remaining payload through.

All modules use logic types, always_ff/always_comb, active-low
synchronous reset, ready/valid handshake with last/keep/user, and
carry full file header comments. Verified clean with iverilog
-g2012 -Wall -t null.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add core streaming RTL modules

Add 7 synthesizable SystemVerilog streaming modules to rtl/core/:

- stream_register_slice: skid-buffer register slice, fully registered
  data and backpressure paths, 1-cycle latency, full throughput
- stream_pipeline_stage: single-stage pipeline register with
  combinational ready path (s_ready = m_ready | ~m_valid)
- stream_skid_buffer: 2-entry EMPTY/ONE/TWO FSM skid buffer, fully
  registered outputs, absorbs 1 cycle of backpressure
- stream_elastic_buffer: parameterizable FIFO-backed elastic buffer,
  registered read port, almost_full/almost_empty flags
- stream_mailbox: 1-deep mailbox register, single-cycle write-to-read
- stream_fifo: synchronous circular FIFO with count/full/empty/
  almost_full/almost_empty status outputs, registered read port
- stream_async_fifo: dual-clock FIFO using Gray-code pointer CDC,
  configurable SYNC_STAGES synchronizers, registered read port

All modules use:
  - snake_case signal names, UPPER_CASE parameters
  - clk / rst_n (active-low synchronous reset) conventions
  - ready/valid handshake with keep/last/user sideband signals
  - logic type, always_ff/always_comb blocks, no initial blocks
  - Full file header comments (description, params, ports, timing)

commit.message:
Movement invariant checker (#172)

* Add movement invariant checker and fix NES boundary prediction

- Add --invariants mode to diagnose.py that checks per-step movement
  invariants: zero-movement, missing wavefront, empty action masks,
  zero PBRS on tile change, and movement prediction mismatches.

- Fix _can_move_from() in frame_skip_wrapper.py to handle NES
  BoundByRoom (Z_01.asm:3505) which blocks movement at UW room
  edges independently of tile walkability.

- Fix _can_move_from() to handle doorway direction constraint:
  when doorway_dir != 0, NES only allows movement in that direction.

- Fix _can_move_from() for gridOffset edge case: when |gridOffset|>=4,
  NES uses ObjDir (not input direction), which may be blocked by
  BoundByRoom even though the input direction would be fine.

- Add 5 NES RAM variables to data.json: doorway_dir, triggered_door_cmd,
  cur_opened_doors, link_obj_timer, link_shove_dir.

Validated across 3 models, 30+ episodes, 0 movement-prediction-wrong.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix cave exit movement: block north until Link steps away

The cave entrance tile (0xF3) is physically unwalkable in the NES —
Walker_CheckTileCollision blocks movement, but PlayerUnwalkable
(Z_07.asm:2952) triggers cave entry via CheckPassiveTileObjects.

Three changes:

1. Remove OW_CAVE_ENTRY_TILES override from is_tile_walkable() in room.py.
   Cave tiles are correctly treated as unwalkable for movement prediction.

2. Add is_cave_entry_direction() to Room and cave entry override in
   ZeldaGame.can_link_move() — allows agent to enter caves by detecting
   cave tiles as a valid action target (but not physically walkable).

3. Track just_exited_cave in FrameSkipWrapper. Set when cave-to-overworld
   transition is detected, cleared after any successful tile movement.
   When set, can_link_move(N) returns False, preventing cave re-entry.

Verified: at cave exit pos (64,77) only S is available. After one S step,
all valid directions unlock. 0 invariant violations across 3 episodes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Delay faulty-thingy TCP connections

Replace the previous random SYN-drop behavior with a deterministic network delay for traffic from the faulty-thingy container.

The startup script now configures tc/netem on eth0 and delays packets sourced from port 8080 by 65 seconds, which makes establishing a connection take longer than 60 seconds. The deployment exposes the delay through CONNECT_DELAY_SECONDS, and the server comments/logging were updated to describe the new behavior accurately.
